title: Bengali Begun Bhaja (Spiced Fried Brinjal)

ingredients.name: large brinjal (eggplant)
visual_checks: choose a large, heavy brinjal for thick rounds
ingredients.notes: choose a large, heavy brinjal for thick rounds

subs.name: thick rounds of ridge gourd or raw banana
subs.notes: both work well with the same spice rub and frying method

subs.name: coconut oil
subs.notes: add 1 extra pinch of turmeric to compensate for the reduced pungency

steps.text: Slice the brinjal into rounds about 1 cm thick. Sprinkle salt generously over both sides and rest on a rack or colander for 8 minutes — moisture will bead on the surface. Pat dry with a cloth.
Mix turmeric and red chilli powder in a small bowl with a pinch of salt. Dust both sides of each brinjal round lightly with the spice mix, pressing gently so it adheres.
Heat mustard oil in a tawa or wide frying pan over medium-high flame until the oil smells nutty and pale smoke appears. Arrange the brinjal rounds in a single layer without overcrowding.

Fry undisturbed for 4 minutes until the underside is deep golden-brown and the rounds have shrunk slightly. Flip carefully and fry the other side for 3 to 4 minutes. The centre should press easily — if it is still firm, reduce the flame and cover for 1 minute.
Remove and serve immediately or at room temperature beside dal-bhaat. Do not stack the pieces on top of each other or the crust will soften.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: brinjal → thick rounds of ridge gourd or raw banana (both work well with the same spice rub and frying method); mustard-oil → coconut oil (add 1 extra pinch of turmeric to compensate for the reduced pungency)
